Since the Commission was not holding the hearing, in terms of the Hon'ble Supreme
Court’s order dated 28.8.2020 in Contempt Petition (C) No. 429/2020 in C.A No. 14697/
2015, the Petitioner approached the Appellate Tribunal for Electricity ('APTEL') vide O.P
No. 4/2021, under Section 121 of the Act.

V AP discoms) filed b...

to adjudicate the disputes between the parties in respect of the PPA dated 31.7.2012, the
other submissions of the applicants namely, that the judgment of the Hon’ble Supreme Court
Order in Petition No. 91/MP/20...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In the light of the above, we hold
that this Commission has the jurisdiction to regulate the tariff of the project of the Respondent,
under Section 79(1)(b) of the Act and to adjudicate the disputes in terms of Section 79(1)(f)
of the Act.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
It is the case of
the Respondent Discoms that a petition for a money claim by a generator against
the State Discoms is not maintainable under Section 79 (1) (f) of the Act.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(b) The ambit of Section 79 (1) (f) of the Act is quite narrow, and the present
dispute cannot be adjudicated by this Commission.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In terms of the judgment
dated 28.8.2024 of the APTEL in Appeal No. 309/2019 (MPPMCL vs DVC), as
confirmed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court vide its order dated 23.9.2024 in C.A
No. 10480/2024 (DVC vs MPPMCL), there is a difference in the language of
Section 79 (1) (f) and Section 86 (1) (f) of the Act,...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(d) Section 79 (1) (f) of the Act relates to the adjudication of disputes involving
the generating companies or transmission licensees in respect of the
determination or regulation of tariff.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Thus, the Central Commission under Section 79 of the Act lacks the
jurisdiction to adjudicate the dispute raised by the Petitioner.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(f) Section 79 of the Act provides for the jurisdiction to adjudicate the dispute
involving the generating companies or the transmission licensees, in relation to
the regulation of tariff under (a) to (c) and the determination of tariff under (d).
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Section 86 (1) (a) and (b) of the Act provides for the power to determine tariff/
regulate tariff.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Section 86 (1) (f) of the Act provides for jurisdiction to adjudicate
disputes between licensees and the distribution company.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Section 86 (1) (f) does
not restrict the jurisdiction of the State Commission to adjudicate disputes relating
solely to the determination/ regulation, as in the case of Section 79 (1) (f), and is
independent of the determination and regulation of tariff.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(g) Under Section 79(1)(f) of the Act, there is no mention of any power of the
Commission to adjudicate a dispute between a distribution licensee and a
generating company.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The dispute in the Energy Watchdog case, involving
generating companies and distribution licensees, concerned a composite
scheme for determining and regulating tariffs as covered under Section 79 (1) (f)
on the grounds of a change in law and force majeure.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(h) Adjudication of disputes between a licensee and a generator is only
provided for under Section 86(1)(f) of the Act.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Section 86 alone
provides for the adjudication of disputes between the generating company and
the licensee on issues other than disputes relating to the determination or
regulation of tariff.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)
